assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and a valid email address they can verify.
No Dwolla Sandbox account, application, or OAuth access token exists yet.
Sandbox-only work is in scope; production approval and live money movement are out of scope.

developer_goal: Create a Dwolla Sandbox account and complete the earliest official first authenticated sandbox API success.

first_success.outcome: Authenticated Root response from the Dwolla sandbox API, including Platform Account links, after token exchange.
first_success.observable_signal: GET https://api-sandbox.dwolla.com/ returns `_links.account.href` (and related HAL links), matching the Quickstart You did it terminal.

route_selection.surface: Dwolla Developer Portal Quickstart plus Sandbox Dashboard credentials
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete sandbox account-to-Root path only after the implementation tab (cURL versus SDK language) is fixed.

candidates.name: Quickstart cURL: client credentials token then Root GET
candidates.reason: Docs show an explicit Basic-auth token POST plus Root GET, but peer TypeScript, Python, PHP, and Ruby tabs are presented without naming cURL as the sole default.

candidates.name: Quickstart SDK tab (TypeScript, Python, PHP, or Ruby)
candidates.reason: SDK tabs change install, env var names, and whether token exchange is a separate developer action versus automatic client caching.

candidates.name: Funds-flow guides after You did it
candidates.reason: The Quickstart explicitly ends at the authenticated Root response and then directs the developer to pick a separate funds flow.

required_path.action: Create a Dwolla Balance sandbox account from the official sandbox signup form.

required_fields.label: Dwolla Developer Terms of Service and Privacy Policy

required_path.observable_result: Dwolla sends a sandbox email verification message.

required_path.action: Open the Dwolla verification email and verify the address.

required_fields.label: Email verification
required_fields.notes: Unverified accounts receive 401 InvalidAccountStatus on API calls.

required_path.observable_result: The developer is redirected to the Sandbox Dashboard.

required_path.action: Open Applications in the Sandbox Dashboard and copy the client_id and client_secret.
required_path.observable_result: Sandbox application credentials are available for token exchange.

required_path.action: Complete the selected Quickstart implementation tab through access-token acquisition and the Root request.

required_fields.label: Implementation tab (cURL or SDK language)
required_fields.notes: cURL shows an explicit token POST; several SDK tabs obtain and cache tokens inside the client.

required_path.observable_result: The Root response includes `_links.account.href` and related HAL links.

prerequisites.description: A local environment able to send HTTPS requests to api-sandbox.dwolla.com, or an installed official SDK for the chosen tab.

external_gates.description: Email verification is required before sandbox API calls succeed.

unavoidable_waits.description: Dwolla must deliver the sandbox signup verification email before dashboard access and API calls work.

platform_outcomes.description: After verification, Dwolla creates the sandbox account, an application, Superhero Savings Bank funding source, and a $5,000 test balance.
A successful client-credentials exchange returns a Bearer access_token.
The Root endpoint returns HAL links including the Platform Account URL.

excluded.item: Reading documentation as a counted action
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence for the path, not a product interaction.

excluded.item: Optional marketing email checkbox on signup
excluded.reason: The public signup form presents keep-me-informed as optional.

excluded.item: Funds-flow guides after You did it
excluded.reason: The Quickstart terminal is the authenticated Root response; funds flows are next steps.
Sandbox signup states it does not guarantee production Platform access.

sources.title: Testing in the Sandbox
evidence_supported: Automatic application and test funding source provisioning

uncertainties.question: Which Quickstart implementation tab (cURL versus TypeScript, Python, PHP, or Ruby) is the canonical shortest first-request route?
uncertainties.impact: Tabs change whether token exchange is a separate developer action, which env vars are required, and whether an SDK install is required.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: An official single default tab or recommendation for the shortest account-to-Root path.

uncertainties.question: Should signup field burden follow the public multi-field form or the Testing guide claim that only a valid email is needed?
uncertainties.impact: Observed signup fields exceed the email-only description and change required_fields counts if verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Confirm whether all observed signup controls remain required on a fresh sandbox registration.
